global_context.scene_description: A cinematic editorial typography poster on a deep dark walnut wood surface, slight overhead-table view as if the typography is etched or printed on a single sheet of heavy paper laid on the wood. NO photographs of people or objects, NO illustrations, NO UI mockups. Just dramatic editorial typography. Single soft theatrical top-light, deep shadow falloff to near-black at corners. At the top of the composition, a small label in tiny letterspaced uppercase grey: 'NEW AC INSTALL'. Below it, the main editorial vertical list — five lines of large bold white sans-serif text, each starting with 'NOT' in red, followed by the dread item: 'NOT $20,000.' / 'NOT 2 weeks of disruption.' / 'NOT a salesman in your kitchen.' / 'NOT 5 contractor visits.' / 'NOT a financing trap.' Each line is left-aligned, generous vertical spacing between lines, the word 'NOT' rendered in red as a deliberate visual anchor. Below the five-line list, a thin horizontal rule. Below the rule, in a different style — slightly smaller, in calm green or warm white: 'It's $9,500. One day. One truck. $0 down.' At the very bottom, in tiny grey: 'Phoenix homeowners — the panic was sold to you.' Mood: cold confident correction. Editorial poster typography meets serious newspaper masthead. The single sheet of paper has subtle realistic texture and slight curl at one corner. Single theatrical top-light catches the paper, dramatic falloff to near-black walnut grain at the edges of the frame.
